description:
You are a performance engineer who must identify slow SQL queries captured in a log file.

    1. A log file already exists at  
       /home/user/app/logs/perf.log

       The file contains exactly six text lines, each ending with a UNIX line-feed (LF) character:

           2023-08-17 10:15:01 INFO Query A Execution time: 123 ms
           2023-08-17 10:15:02 INFO Query B Execution time: 567 ms
           2023-08-17 10:15:03 INFO Query C Execution time: 754 ms
           2023-08-17 10:15:04 INFO Query D Execution time: 31 ms
           2023-08-17 10:15:05 INFO Query E Execution time: 499 ms
           2023-08-17 10:15:06 INFO Query F Execution time: 820 ms

    2. Any query that took 500 ms or more is considered “slow”.
       For this exercise a line qualifies as slow if it matches the regular-expression

           Execution time: [5-9][0-9]{2} ms

       • The pattern looks for “Execution time: ” followed by
         a three-digit number whose first digit is 5–9, and the suffix “ ms”.
       • Only full log lines that satisfy this pattern should be collected.

    3. Your task: using a single terminal command (pipelines are fine so long as they are written on one line) extract all slow-query lines from  
       /home/user/app/logs/perf.log  
       and write them to a new file  

       /home/user/output/slow_queries.log

    4. Output file requirements:
       • The file path must be exactly /home/user/output/slow_queries.log  
       • It must contain only the qualifying lines, in the same order they appear in the source log.  
       • Each line must end with a single LF character.  
       • No extra whitespace, blank lines, headers, or footers are allowed.

    5. When you are done, you can display the contents of /home/user/output/slow_queries.log so the result is visible, but this is optional.
truth:
Initial-state prerequisites:
      • Directory /home/user/app/logs/ exists (0755)  
      • Directory /home/user/output/ exists (0755) and is empty  
      • File /home/user/app/logs/perf.log (0644) with the exact six lines:

          2023-08-17 10:15:01 INFO Query A Execution time: 123 ms\n
          2023-08-17 10:15:02 INFO Query B Execution time: 567 ms\n
          2023-08-17 10:15:03 INFO Query C Execution time: 754 ms\n
          2023-08-17 10:15:04 INFO Query D Execution time: 31 ms\n
          2023-08-17 10:15:05 INFO Query E Execution time: 499 ms\n
          2023-08-17 10:15:06 INFO Query F Execution time: 820 ms\n

    Post-task verification:
      • File /home/user/output/slow_queries.log must exist (0644)  
      • It must contain exactly the following three lines, each terminated by LF and nothing else:

          2023-08-17 10:15:02 INFO Query B Execution time: 567 ms\n
          2023-08-17 10:15:03 INFO Query C Execution time: 754 ms\n
          2023-08-17 10:15:06 INFO Query F Execution time: 820 ms\n
